tossups.question: Former Musee des Beaux Arts-Nice curator Jean Forneris masterminded a 1998 plan to steal this artist’s Cliffs at Dieppe, the first of two times that painting was stolen. He depicted a number of poles next to to the Santa Maria della Salute in a series of paintings of the Grand Canal in Venice. A faceless woman in a spreading white dress sits behind this artist in a John Singer Sargent depiction of him (*) “painting at the edge of a wood”. His late-life cataracts influenced the production of a series of paintings made in his Giverny flower garden. He included a surprisingly non-luminant sun in a depiction of the harbor of Le Havre that, via critic Louis Leroy, gave its name to a movement he pioneered. For 10 points, name this painter of series on Haystacks and Water Lilies who also created Impression, Sunrise.
tossups.answer: Claude Monet [or Oscar-Claude Monet; accept Alfred Sisley until “Cliffs” is read]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Claude Monet [or Oscar-Claude Monet; accept Alfred Sisley until "Cliffs" is read]

A faceless woman in a spreading white dress sits behind this artist in a John Singer Sargent depiction of him (*) "painting at the edge of a wood". His late-life cataracts influenced the production of a series of paintings made in his Giverny flower garden. He included a surprisingly non-luminant sun in a depiction of the harbor of Le Havre that, via critic Louis Leroy, gave its name to a movement he pioneered. For 10 points, name this painter of series on Haystacks and Water Lilies who also created Impression, Sunrise.

bonuses.leadin: This artist included several views of the Thames in his Hampton Court series. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painter, who depicted water-covered streets in the titular French village in his Flood at Port-Marly.
Sisley painted several works near the Forest of Fontainebleau, which was home to this group of pre-Impressionists led by Jean-Francois Millet and Theodore Rousseau.
Several artists from this country worked on the Palace of Fontainebleau, such as Rosso Fiorentino and Benvenuto Cellini.

bonuses.leadin: This man created a Portrait of Painter Bazille and in his old age, he hired Richard Guino to follow his sculpting instructions. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this French painter of Luncheon of the Boating Party.

This other French painter was a longtime owner of Renoir’s Moulin de la Galette, and that painting can be seen in this man’s self-portrait. He is perhaps more famous for The Floor Scrapers and Paris Street: Rainy Day.
Renoir also painted this Impressionist painter of The Boat During the Flood, Port-Marly. This English painter also created a series of paintings of the Thames.

parts_sanitized: Name this French painter of Luncheon of the Boating Party.

bonuses.leadin: This artist’s later pointillist works include Picking Peas and Potato Harvest, and he gained experience while studying under Anton Melbye. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who painted day and night versions of Boulevard Montmartre. His Apple Picking at Eragny and Bather in the Woods both show women in a rural setting.
Pissarro exhibited as part of this movement in the middle of his career. It includes Manet and Monet, whose depiction of a sunrise scene shares its name.
This artist’s earliest surviving work is his Lane Near a Small Town. A British citizen, he is best known for works such as a View of the Thames and his Bridge at Villeneuve-la-Garenne.
